Transcrição Completa: 56 Majority Hook Mastery 1 25 25
[00:00]
"Okay. So, when you want to convert a majority of a market in the most cost-effective way possible, you have to be able to lean into what's called the majority hook."
[00:08]
"Okay. So, first of all, let's just understand the concept of this and then from there we'll dig into the hows and the tactics of finding the hook and developing the hook and leveraging that hook."
[00:18]
"So, first of all, the majority hook is a specific set of messaging or an angle that you found that converts the most people above and beyond every other hook that you've ever tested or tried. Okay?"
[00:30]
"And this specific hook type is going to be your most cost-effective way of converting customers, give or take what market you're converting. Okay?"
[00:38]
"Most businesses, they test and they iterate their way to a point where they eventually find like what we call second and third order hooks."
[00:46]
"Second and third order hooks are like, look, you found something that works. It's doing all right. You know, your cost per call or your cost per lead or your cost per result or whatever."
[00:56]
"It's not the best it could be, but like you know, hey, you're getting results and you're happy with it and you're ROI positive, but you still haven't found like the chosen one."
[01:08]
"You know, the chosen one, the majority hook is going to fucking print for you."
[01:13] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study: Cody Sanchez - Business Acquisition Expert"
NÚMEROS: $10K offer, $35K offer
VISUAL: Layout com dois produtos distintos, destaque para os valores dos programas, estrutura de ofertas escalonadas
[01:15]
"So, let me give you some examples with a client. I have this client, her name's Cody Sanchez. Cody Sanchez teaches people how to buy businesses. She has two different offers."
[01:24]
"There's a 10K offer and there's a 35K offer. I'm going to talk specifically about the 10K offer originally because that's what I've marketed for her the most over the years so far."
[01:34]
"The 35K is relatively new. We're currently trying to test and find the majority hooks for the new 35K offer."
[01:42]
"So, I'll walk through what we did initially to find the majority hook for the 10K product first."
[01:48]
"So, buying a business, there are so many different reasons and so many different hooks available to us to leverage."
[01:55]
"I'm just going to wrap off a fuckload of random ones that we thought through first, turned into ads, advertised, and then watched for what the cost per result was, how warm the leads were that came in as a result of it, how sold people were as a result of the love of leveraging specific hooks and where I think they fell in to this specific order here on the hook scale."
[02:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hook Testing Hierarchy"
NÚMEROS: Ranking de 1º a 3º lugar
VISUAL: Escala visual mostrando diferentes níveis de hooks - Majority Hook (1º), Second Order Hook (2º), Third Order Hook (3º), layout hierárquico com setas indicando performance
[02:20]
"Okay, so for starters, it's like when you're considering buying a business, you could just say it's easier to buy a business than start one. Bam. As a hook, that shit crushed, okay?"
[02:30]
"It did great, but it didn't do the best. Okay, didn't do the best comparatively to what we eventually found."
[02:36]
"So, that became a second order hook. It became like a minority hook, okay? it was responsible and still today is responsible for a good chunk of customers that come through, but it's not the chosen one."
[02:47]
"You know, an example of some other small ones that make a lot of sense. Okay, so when you go and you buy a business, you're essentially buying like all their existing infrastructure, their tech, their SOPs, their systems, their staff, their physical locations, their supplies, their inventory."
[03:03]
"Okay, so it's also a way to kind of hypers scale."
[03:07] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Third Order Hook Example: Infrastructure Acquisition"
NÚMEROS: 3º lugar no ranking
VISUAL: Ícones representando diferentes ativos de negócio - pessoas, sistemas, inventário, localização física, relacionamentos, layout de grade mostrando multiplicidade de ativos
[03:09]
"So the example of a hook and we can articulate this in a lot of different ways which is an example of why I use it. Okay."
[03:15]
"So we would start off with saying something along the lines of like when you buy a business you're buying immediate access to a stream of customers to inventory relationships to manufacturing partners to vendors to staff and like we would just list all that same shit I just said."
[03:30]
"Now that hook was good but again wasn't the best. It was probably our third order hook that we found. Okay. came in third place."
[03:38]
"Then we found a bunch of small variations of that. Like another example of a small variation that's along the same lines of that same hook is when you buy a business, you are also immediately buying a lump sum of customers that are ready to transact with you."
[03:52]
"Okay. Well, that kind of makes sense, but I'm sure that you can relate to the fact that there's probably some explanation needed with that. It's not necessarily as simple as what I just made it sound. I have to further elaborate on it. It doesn't just immediately land with people. Okay."
[04:06] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "THE MAJORITY HOOK: Boomers Are Dying"
NÚMEROS: 1º lugar - Hook vencedor
VISUAL: Destaque visual dramático, fonte grande e impactante, ícone de troféu ou medalha de ouro indicando primeiro lugar, contraste visual forte para enfatizar a descoberta principal
[04:08]
"Now, through testing, what Cody eventually found as the majority hook, okay, the hook that made the most sense, that landed with the most people, was this hook of boomers are dying and their businesses are going to die with them if somebody doesn't come along and take them over. That could be you."
[04:28]
"That hook, for some reason, fucking crushes. That became the majority hook. that came in first place. Okay."
[04:36]
"And that specific hook we can leverage from a few different angles. We can attack it from multiple perspectives."
[04:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Dead Tree Story: Multiple Perspectives Concept"
NÚMEROS: 100 passos de distância mencionados
VISUAL: Ilustração conceitual de uma árvore vista de diferentes ângulos, representação visual de perspectiva múltipla, Colorado mountains como cenário, contraste entre árvores vivas e mortas
[04:44]
"I was on a walk with my grandfather in the Colorado mountains one time and there was this little beetle that was eating the pine trees from the inside out and it would kill the trees."
[04:55]
"So, you're looking on this walk here on a on a mountain trail and like just swaths of trees that are dead, you know? Like, picture dead pine trees. None of the little needles on them, just fucking wood standing there, eaten from the inside out."
[05:09]
"And we get to an area of the forest where we're in a dense, healthy area of pine trees. And there ends up being one particular tree in front of us that I was looking at that uh was like it looked dead, but it was in a sea of healthy trees."
[05:23]
"And I pointed out to my grandfather, I'm like, 'Hey, look at that tree over there. Is that one of those trees? Like is that one getting attacked by the beetles?' And he was like, 'Maybe, you know, who knows?'"
[05:33]
"So anyway, we walk past the tree and we get another like hundred steps further. Okay, not that far at all. And we're hiking up a mountain. So, we're getting some elevation as we climb."
[05:43]
"And uh I turned around and I wanted to see like were there other trees that were dying as well now that we had a slightly better view."
[05:50]
"And when I turned around, like I couldn't even see the tree anymore. It was if that one dead tree had just vanished and was gone."
[05:57]
"And that didn't make any sense to me. And I I don't know why, but I kind of like tripped out about this. I was like, 'Dude, what the fuck happened to that dead tree? Like, we're like barely any further. Like, it can't be hidden behind a bunch of other trees. Like, it should be pretty obvious to see. It was very tall.'"
[06:14]
"And uh my grandfather goes, 'I mean, maybe it was only dead from that one angle.'"
[06:19]
"And I was like, 'No way, dude. Like, how can a tree only be dead from one angle? Like, that can't be true.' And he was like, 'No, dude. Like, that's not how it works.'"
[06:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Perspective Principle: Same Object, Different Angles"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Demonstração visual de um objeto (Apple Pen) visto de múltiplos ângulos, comparação lado a lado mostrando perspectiva frontal vs lateral, setas indicando diferentes pontos de vista
[06:29]
"And he holds up, he held up his finger. Okay, I'll use his Apple pen as an example for you. And he says, 'Uh, look, man. Like, you're looking at it.'"
[06:38]
"And he's standing across me. So he's like he's like look you're looking at it from this angle and like you saw it dead initially and versus you like you're looking at it from this angle here and it could have been alive and well from the other side and your angle is completely different from my angle and my angle is completely different from your angle and literally looking at the exact same thing."
[07:00]
"We see completely different things although it's the exact same thing and it's like from different angles you know you could describe something and I could describe something and it seems completely different you know but in reality it's the exact same thing."
[07:14]
"So, it's like if you described your angle on this thing right now and I described my angle, you'd be like, 'Oh, it looks like a pointy, I don't know, almost like a pencil.' And I'd be like, 'No, definitely doesn't look like that.'"
[07:24]
"You know, looks like a flat kind of semi-rounded half circle on my end on a stick. Don't know what you're talking about. And you'd be like, 'Well, I don't know what the fuck you're talking about.'"
[07:33]
"My point is, both of us can see the same thing, the same exact object from a completely different view. And that's what you eventually want to do with majority hooks. Okay?"
[07:43] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Majority Hook Variations: Boomers Dying - Multiple Angles"
NÚMEROS: Dozen (12+) diferentes ângulos testados, 50%+ de conversão consistente
VISUAL: Layout de grade mostrando múltiplas variações do mesmo hook central, cada variação em um card separado, destaque para a consistência de performance acima de 50%
[07:45]
"So for Cody, we would take that same hook from the boomers or dying hook and we milked it from like a dozen different angles, but every time we hit that same hook, it would always convert more than half of the total customer base comparatively to any other hook that we could use."
[08:02]
"So after you find the majority hook, it's not like you just simply put say it that one particular way. You say it every possible way that you can look at that same thing and give as many different perspectives on the same object as possible."
[08:16] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hook Variation #1: Succession Opportunity"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Texto do hook em destaque, visual de transferência de negócio, ícone de sucessão empresarial
[08:18]
"So, we'd be like, 'Boomers are dying and their businesses are dying with them.' You could be the person who comes in and takes it over."
[08:26] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hook Variation #2: Retirement Support Angle"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Ícone de aposentadoria, representação visual de fluxo de caixa para aposentados, tema de segurança financeira
[08:28]
"We would also use the hook of like, 'Boomers are dying and they need somebody to come and run their businesses so they still have some money in retirement.'"
[08:36] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hook Variation #3: Wealth Transfer Opportunity"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Gráfico de transferência de riqueza geracional, ícones representando negócios e dinheiro, visual de oportunidade urgente
[08:38]
"We'd hit another angle. Boomers are going to die soon. They're some of the richest people on earth and they're rich because of their businesses. And if somebody doesn't come along and take over their businesses, their businesses are going to die with them."
[08:52]
"Somebody needs to come in and create an opportunity to get some cash to those businesses through their some cash to those boomers in their retirement from those businesses."
[09:01]
"They might even give their businesses away to you if you just give them a fraction of the cash flow that comes in as a result."
[09:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hook Variation #4: Cash Flow Share Model"
NÚMEROS: Fração do cash flow mencionada
VISUAL: Diagrama de divisão de cash flow, representação visual de acordo win-win, ícones de parceria
[09:10]
"We'd use that exact end of the hook at the beginning of the hook where we'd be like, 'Boomers are dying and they're willing to give their business to you if you just give them a little bit of the cash flow that you generate from it to them through their retirement to help keep them comfortable going into the ground.'"
[09:27]
"You know, like we would use all kinds of random shit like that and just attack the same exact thing from so many different perspectives until eventually what we found is again time and time again, no matter how much we tested against it, that one hook absolutely crushed it for us. Okay."
[09:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Product Differentiation: $10K vs $35K Offer"
NÚMEROS: $10K produto para iniciantes, $35K produto para empresários estabelecidos
VISUAL: Comparação lado a lado dos dois produtos, diferentes avatares de cliente, setas indicando diferentes níveis de sofisticação
[09:46]
"But again, that one hook specifically worked extremely well for one of our products that was 10K. And that specific product was catered not necessarily towards beginners or noobs by any means, but it was catered towards a demographic that like hadn't bought a business yet."
[10:02]
"Might have not been making like the most money. Could potentially even be new to business and just again buying a business makes more sense than building one."
[10:11]
"So again, that worked out really well and a lot of people would buy that from that angle."
[10:16]
"However, that new 35K product I told you about that we just came out with, that new one, when we use the Boomer hook for that doesn't relate as well."
[10:26]
"It actually kind of feels like a minority hook when we advertise to that demographic because that offer in that demographic type is towards a much much more serious business owner."
[10:37] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "$35K Offer Target: Scaling Through Acquisitions"
NÚMEROS: $35K investimento, múltiplo de saída aumentado
VISUAL: Estratégia de roll-up visual, diagrama de aquisições múltiplas, gráfico de crescimento de EBITDA, representação de enterprise value
[10:39]
"It's towards a business owner that is in a position that wants to scale their business through acquisitions. Okay?"
[10:46]
"That specific business owner wants to find a way to acquire companies, roll them up, exit for more money than what they'd get on their own business if they tried to sell it solo. Okay?"
[10:57]
"And they want to create more enterprise value, more IBIDA for themselves to get a bigger multiple and a bigger exit potential just from rolling up businesses."
[11:07] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "New Hook Testing: Bulk Customer Acquisition"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Comparação visual entre aquisição de clientes um a um (advertising) vs em massa (business acquisition), gráfico de eficiência, ícones representando múltiplos clientes
[11:09]
"Some of those specific businesses for that 35k product, the hooks that we found that have worked so far, as an example, they just want to acquire customers in bulk at once."
[11:19]
"So, it's like, you know how advertising, like what you and I are talking about right now, technically acquires customers one at a time. Well, through buying a business, it's like you're just acquiring all the customers from that specific business that they have in a single transaction."
[11:34]
"Some people prefer that instead of advertising and getting one customer at a time."
[11:38]
"So, it's like that's an example of a hook that's also worked really well for us. The are you advertising and getting a single customer at a time? Why wouldn't you just buy a business and get a bulk amount of customers at once?"
[11:50]
"You see, it's like there's all kinds of little hooks that we're currently going through the testing phase."
[11:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Finding The Majority Hook: The Testing Process"
NÚMEROS: Fase de testes em andamento
VISUAL: Funil de testes, múltiplas variações sendo testadas simultaneamente, métricas de performance, processo iterativo visual
[11:57]
"And this is the most important thing to understand about how you find the majority hook. And this is really all it comes down to. It is testing a wide range of ideas."
[12:07]
"You test and iterate your way to finding the majority hook. And you want to find the majority hook. The majority hook brings in the hottest people."
[12:16] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Majority Hook Results: High-Intent Leads"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Representação de leads "quentes" vs "frios", termômetro de intenção, ícones de clientes altamente qualificados
[12:18]
"Like in that Cody example, the people who show up to a call that say, 'Dude, I've got a business in mind right now that's run by a baby boomer and they look like they're going to die and I I already have marketing skills and I have sales skills and I really think I could come into that business, run it for them, get the cash flow from it, give them some cash flow through their retirement, and then eventually when they pass away, you know, I just get the business in in in its entirety. I don't even think I'm going to have to come out of pocket for that.'"
[12:48]
"It's like that person's so sold on the idea. It's just it's almost as if a majority hook and an inmarket demographic can like combine together for a superpower like the wonder twins, you know?"
[13:00]
"It's so effective to leverage majority hooks."
[13:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "WRONG WAY: Testing Only First 3-5 Seconds"
NÚMEROS: 3-5 segundos, meses de testes incorretos
VISUAL: X vermelho grande sobre a prática errada, comparação visual do que NÃO fazer, destaque em vermelho para erro crítico
[13:05]
"But this is the issue, right? Like I had this consulting client, this was like just devastating when I found out this was their way of doing ad creative testing."
[13:14]
"They would only test the first like 3 to 5 seconds of the ad and it would barely be different. The body of the the entire ad video is the same for like months they had done this."
[13:25]
"The only thing they had done this is before they were working with me. When I found out about it, that's when I was devastated. Just felt irresponsible to run abs this way."
[13:34]
"They were exclusively taking that first three to five seconds and changing the hook from like one minority hook to like a slightly different minority hook."
[13:42]
"Like their way of testing was like changing some of the B-roll. You know, they wouldn't change the actual messaging or the hook of the ad."
[13:49] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hook vs Body Structure"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Diagrama de estrutura de anúncio - Hook (topo) + Body (meio/base), setas mostrando relação entre elementos, destaque para importância de ambos
[13:51]
"Remember, the hook is the opener. Okay, that's the one variation I want you to understand here that's important to get. But the body of the ad should also support the opening hook."
[14:02]
"See, the body is like the reasoning that the hook makes sense. That's why people will pay attention to the rest of the ad."
[14:09]
"The hook will get them to pay attention and come in framed a particular way. But again, the body does the justifying for the opening hook."
[14:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "BEST PRACTICE: Test Complete Ads"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Checkmark verde grande, representação de ad completo sendo testado (hook + body), selo de "best practice"
[14:20]
"So don't be the type of person that leaves the body the exact same in every video. A incredible practice like I've never done that with Cody as an example or any one of our top clients for that matter."
[14:32]
"We always film unique ads through and through. Okay? It's just a best practice of ours."
[14:37]
"Have I seen people test just the beginning and leave the body the same and still seen it work? Yes. Just telling you what I've done and what's worked for us and our clients at scale."
[14:47]
"You can do it the other way if you find success with that. Of course, I kind of think that of that is like lazy advertising."
[14:54]
"And I'm not again I'm not hating on it in the instance that you're getting success with it. I want to make money at the end of the day. I don't want to be right."
[15:02]
"As soon as that becomes the right way to do it, you'll get an updated video from me telling you to do just that."
[15:08]
"But for now, what we consistently see work is leverage the hook as the main thing you're testing, but always follow it up with a body of the ad that helps justify and sell why that hook makes sense."
[15:21] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How You Know You Found The Majority Hook"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Checklist de indicadores de sucesso, gráficos de performance melhorando, ícones representando diferentes métricas positivas
[15:23]
"So anyway, that's all I've really got for you here. The majority hook makes a ton of sense. You have to find it."
[15:29]
"And you'll know you found it to be clear when you start to get a far greater response. You'll see people come in more enthused."
[15:36]
"You'll see your cost per whatever you're optimizing for drop. You'll typically see your rorowaz lift and and more importantly, you'll see the longevity of the hook as you scale into it last a lot longer."
[15:49] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Majority Hook vs Minority Hook Longevity"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Gráfico de linha comparativo mostrando longevidade - majority hook mantém performance ao escalar, minority hooks decaem rapidamente, curvas contrastantes
[15:51]
"There's a lot more longevity to majority hooks. Minority hooks kind of frivolous, you know, like they feel short-lived. They die out rapidly."
[16:00]
"Even if they have signs of life that are similar to the cost per result of the majority hook, they as soon as you try to scale into them, like the cost per result just goes all out of fucking whack, you know?"
[16:11]
"Like you don't get the actual result you're after, which is scaling into something and making a fuckload of money. You just make like a little bit of money off of it."
[16:19] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hook Portfolio Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Majority Hook + Second Order Hook para escala
VISUAL: Portfólio de hooks em camadas, destaque para majority e second order como escaláveis, minority hooks como complemento
[16:21]
"Which, to be fair, I want to let you know, I mean, we don't just do the majority hook. Like, we still run ads with all these other minority hooks, too, but we don't get the opportunity to scale with them."
[16:32]
"You only get really scale with the majority hook and the second order hook. That's the key here. It's a scaling tactic."
[16:39]
"All right, thanks for watching. See you in the next video."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Oferta de $10K (produto para iniciantes em aquisição de negócios)
Oferta de $35K (produto para empresários estabelecidos focados em roll-ups)
Conversão de 50%+ consistente com majority hook
Aproximadamente 12+ variações do mesmo hook testadas
3-5 segundos (tempo de teste inadequado mencionado como erro)
100 passos de distância (história da árvore morta)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Majority Hook: Hook que converte a maioria do mercado de forma mais custo-efetiva
Second Order Hook: Hooks que funcionam bem mas não são os mais eficazes
Third Order Hook: Hooks que têm performance menor ainda
Minority Hooks: Hooks com menor performance e longevidade
Hook Testing Process: Testar ampla variedade de ideias e iterar até encontrar o vencedor
Multiple Perspective Strategy: Atacar o mesmo hook de múltiplos ângulos (como a história da árvore)
Complete Ad Testing: Testar hook + body completo, não apenas primeiros segundos
Hook Portfolio: Manter majority hook para escala + minority hooks para diversificação
ESTRUTURA:
Hook (abertura) + Body (justificativa e suporte)
Body deve justificar por que o hook faz sentido
Nunca deixar o body igual enquanto testa apenas o hook
Filmar anúncios únicos completos para cada teste
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Produto $10K: Para quem ainda não comprou negócio, pode ser novo em business
Produto $35K: Para empresários sérios querendo escalar via aquisições
Cost per lead/call/result: Métrica principal de otimização
ROAS (Return on Ad Spend): Aumenta com majority hook
Longevidade de escala: Majority hooks escalam melhor e por mais tempo
CASE STUDY - CODY SANCHEZ:
Hooks Testados (em ordem de performance):
1º LUGAR - MAJORITY HOOK: "Boomers estão morrendo e seus negócios vão morrer com eles"
Variações do Majority Hook:
Ângulo de sucessão: Você pode assumir o negócio
Ângulo de aposentadoria: Boomers precisam de alguém para gerar renda na aposentadoria
Ângulo de transferência de riqueza: Boomers são ricos por seus negócios que morrerão com eles
Ângulo de cash flow share: Boomers darão o negócio por fração do cash flow
2º LUGAR - SECOND ORDER HOOK: "É mais fácil comprar um negócio do que começar um"
3º LUGAR - THIRD ORDER HOOK: "Ao comprar um negócio, você compra infraestrutura completa" (tech, SOPs, staff, locations, inventory, customers, vendors, manufacturing partners)
HOOKS PARA $35K OFFER (em teste):
"Aquisição de clientes em massa vs um de cada vez via advertising"
Roll-up strategy para aumentar EBITDA e múltiplo de saída
Criar enterprise value através de aquisições
INDICADORES DE MAJORITY HOOK ENCONTRADO: ✓ Maior resposta do público ✓ Leads mais entusiasmados ✓ Cost per result reduz significativamente ✓ ROAS aumenta ✓ Longevidade ao escalar é muito maior ✓ Performance consistente acima de 50% de conversão ✓ Não deteriora ao escalar (diferente de minority hooks)
DURAÇÃO: ~16:39 FORMATO: Masterclass educacional em vídeo sobre estratégia de marketing
